Bug / Task Description: Truncating the write-ahead log reports the correct number of removed entries, but afterwards the log retains exactly the entries that were supposed to be discarded and drops the ones that should survive. Replaying after truncation returns stale, already-checkpointed records and loses the newest ones, breaking crash-recovery semantics. The returned removed-count looks right, hiding the cause.
Commit Subject: fix(wal): keep the entries after the truncation point
